zh.Eastnor, Herefordshire, England: 英國賀福爾易亞斯特諾
zh.Leominster, Herefordshire, England: 英國賀福爾萊奧明斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Clynton於大約1514年出生在英國賀福爾易亞斯特諾時，他的父親William Clynton26歲，母親Elizabeth Walwyn27歲。他和Margery Tracy至少有3個兒子和7個女兒。他去世於1575年12月22日在英國享年62歲。

zh-hans.Leominster, Herefordshire, England: 英国賀福爾萊奧明斯特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Clynton于大约1514年出生在英国賀福爾易亞斯特諾时，他的父亲William Clynton26岁，母亲Elizabeth Walwyn27岁。他和Margery Tracy至少有3个儿子和7个女儿。他去世于1575年12月22日在英国享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clynton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Louth, Westmeath) and Scottish: shortened form of   McClinton  .

English: habitational name, either from Glympton in Oxfordshire, named as ‘settlement (Old English tūn) on the Glym river’, a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright stream’, or from Glinton in Northamptonshire, early recorded as Clinton (named with an unrecorded Old English element akin to Middle Low German glinde ‘enclosure, fence’ + Old English tūn). The surname was taken to Ireland in or before the 16th century.

History: Charles Clinton (born 1690 in Longford, Ireland) organized a group of colonists and founded the settlement of Little Britain, Ulster County, NY, in 1731. His son George Clinton (1739–1812) was governor of NY (1777–95), and they had many prominent descendants. — An English aristocratic family called Clinton was founded by Geoffrey de Clinton (flourished 1130), who held lands at Glympton, Oxfordshire. He was chamberlain and treasurer to Henry I (1100–35).

variants.name: McClinton
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Fhionntain (Scottish), Mac Giolla Fhionntáin (Irish) ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Fionntan’, whose name has been explained as ‘the white ancient’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Clynton was born about 1514, in Eastnor, Herefordshire, England, his father, William Clynton, was 26 and his mother, Elizabeth Walwyn, was 27. He had at least 3 sons and 7 daughters with Margery Tracy. He died on 22 December 1575, in England, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Clynton est né environ 1514, à Eastnor, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, William Clynton, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Walwyn, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 7 filles avec Margery Tracy. Il est décédé le 22 décembre 1575, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Clynton um 1514 in Eastnor, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Clynton 26 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Walwyn 27. Er hatte mit Margery Tracy mindestens 3 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 22. Dezember 1575 im Alter von 62 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Clynton è nato il circa 1514 nel Eastnor, Herefordshire, England, il suo padre, William Clynton, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Walwyn, aveva 27 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 7 figlie con Margery Tracy. Morì il 22 dicembre 1575, nel England, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.Ledbury, Herefordshire, England: イギリスハーエフォードシュアイアレドブリー

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Clyntonが約1514年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアエアストノーで生まれた時，父William Clyntonは26歳，母Elizabeth Walwynは27歳であった。彼はMargery Tracyとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と7人の娘をもうけた。彼は1575年12月22日にイギリスで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Clynton(이)가 대략 1514년 영국 허퍼드실르 이스트너에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Clynton(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Walwyn(은)는 27세였다. 그는 Margery Tracy(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1575년 12월 22일 영국에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Clynton nasceu aproximadamente 1514, em Eastnor, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Clynton, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Walwyn, tinha 27 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 7 filhas com Margery Tracy. Ele faleceu em 22 de dezembro de 1575, em Inglaterra, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Clynton родился около 1514 г. в местности эастнор, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Clynton было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Walwyn, 27. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 7 дочерей от Margery Tracy. Он умер 22 декабря 1575 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Clynton nació aproximadamente 1514, en Eastnor, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Clynton, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Walwyn, tenía 27 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 7 hijas con Margery Tracy. Murió el 22 de diciembre de 1575, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años.
